affected_versions: IntelliVue patient monitors MX100, MX400-MX850, MP2-MP90, IntelliVue X2/X3 (Version N and prior); Patient Information Center iX B.02/C.02/C.03; PerformanceBridge Focal Point A.01
description: The device does not sufficiently prove an identity claim over the adjacent network, allowing an attacker on the hospital network segment to impersonate a trusted actor and read/modify/deny monitor data.
mitigation: Physically or logically isolate the patient-monitoring network segment from the general hospital LAN; restrict adjacent-network access to authorized hosts only (network-layer micro-segmentation, not a device-side patch).

affected_versions: IntelliVue patient monitors MX100, MX400-MX850, MP2-MP90, IntelliVue X2/X3 (Version N and prior); Patient Information Center iX B.02/C.02/C.03; PerformanceBridge Focal Point A.01
cwe: CWE-1286 Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input
description: Malformed input to the certificate enrollment service from an adjacent network can crash the service, blocking new device enrollment (monitoring of already-enrolled devices continues).
mitigation: Restrict adjacent-network reachability to the enrollment service; monitor for repeated malformed enrollment attempts.

affected_versions: OpenSSH through 7.7 (bundled sshd on many embedded/IIoT network stacks)
cwe: CWE-362 Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization
description: OpenSSH through 7.7 allows remote username enumeration via inconsistent authentication-failure timing, aiding follow-on credential attacks against any exposed management SSH service.
mitigation: Deny inbound port 22 at the network layer for devices that do not require interactive administrative access from the hospital network.

description: scp allows command injection via shell metacharacters in a filename/destination argument. Marked DISPUTED by the OpenSSH vendor, who considers the behavior intentional, but it remains a tracked NVD entry and a real risk if scp is scripted against untrusted filenames.
mitigation: Deny inbound/outbound port 22 at the network layer rather than relying on client-side filename sanitization.

affected_versions: IntelliVue MP Series (MP2/X2/MP30/MP50/MP70/NP90/MX700/MX800) Rev B-M, includes B.01; MX400-550 Rev J-M; X3/MX100 Rev M
description: Unauthenticated same-subnet attacker can write-what-where at a chosen device address, enabling memory read/write and a DoS system restart. Lateral-movement risk from any compromised host on the shared clinical VLAN.
mitigation: Micro-segment the monitor so no untrusted host shares its subnet; allow only documented ports 24105/UDP and 24005/UDP, deny all other adjacent reachability. Network-layer control, no device patch required.
